behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources
behaviors.justification: Unit tests with mocked fetch functions can verify code paths, but real failures occur at the data-collection boundary: wrong endpoint URL, authentication failures, response format changes, or endpoint unavailability. These failures are invisible to unit mocks. HTTP-level test doubles are needed to catch integration issues like malformed responses, timeouts, or auth rejections.
behaviors.test_description: Create test doubles for the metrics endpoint, issue tracker API, and deployment log. Verify each is called with correct parameters, responses are parsed correctly, and the combined data object containing all three data sources is passed to the summary function. Include malformed and partial responses.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a 'data unavailable' note for that section rather than aborting
behaviors.justification: Unit mocks can simulate exceptions, but real endpoint failures manifest differently: TCP timeouts, HTTP 500+ errors, malformed JSON responses, or connection resets. The error-handling code path for a timeout might differ from its path for a 503 error. Need HTTP-level failure simulation to verify the script handles diverse real-world failure modes correctly without crashing.
behaviors.test_description: Set up test doubles for each data source that simulate realistic failure modes: connection timeout, HTTP 500, malformed response, and connection reset. For each failure mode, trigger the script and verify: (1) a report is produced, (2) the report includes an 'unavailable' or 'error' note for that section, (3) the script exits cleanly, (4) other sections' data is present.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data
behaviors.justification: The problem specifies 'narrative summary,' implying LLM-generated prose. LLM outputs are non-deterministic—the same data might produce multiple valid summaries with different phrasings. Unit tests with fixed assertions ('assert 'uptime' in summary') cannot verify semantic accuracy. Testing 'accurately represents' requires calling a real LLM and using semantic validation (e.g., embedding similarity, structured extraction) to verify the narrative preserves and interprets key metrics correctly.
behaviors.test_description: Provide structured test data with specific metrics (e.g., uptime: 99.9%, errors: 42, critical issues: 3). Call the LLM-based summary generator. Extract key numeric and categorical values from the generated narrative using semantic parsing or embedding-based similarity. Verify that the narrative's characterization of these values is accurate (e.g., if uptime is high, the narrative should reflect positive service health; if errors are high, it should note degradation).

behaviors.description: A complete report—from data collection through email delivery—reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end failure boundary that requires real email service interaction. Integration-level mocks of the email service would hide real-world failures: SMTP authentication failures, rate limiting, spam filtering, malformed SMTP commands, TLS issues, or misconfigured recipient addresses. The criterion 'reaches inbox correctly' is fundamentally a System-level question requiring actual email delivery and inbox verification.
behaviors.test_description: Run the full script with test data (either real internal endpoints or realistic test doubles). Include a real LLM call for narrative generation. Send the report to a test email account (e.g., a test Gmail inbox or temporary email service). Verify: (1) email is received, (2) subject and content are correct, (3) all three data sources are represented in the summary, (4) report is readable and formatted properly.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run
behaviors.justification: Requires real filesystem I/O (not mocked) to verify the report is actually persisted with correct content, and HTTP-level email failure simulation (not just exception mocks) to catch realistic service-down scenarios. Unit mocks of file and email operations would hide real failures: permission denied on the save directory, disk full, file handle leaks, or the retry logic failing to read the persisted file on the next run.
behaviors.test_description: Configure the script to save reports to a test directory. Set up a test double for the email service that simulates unreachability (connection timeout or refused). Trigger the script, verify: (1) a report file is written to the configured path with correct contents, (2) the script logs the failure, (3) on a second run, the script attempts redelivery of the persisted report, (4) after successful redelivery, the file is cleaned up or marked as delivered.
